posting.jobTitle: Linoleum, Resilient Tile, and Carpet Layer Apprentice
posting.sponsorName: Joint Apprenticeship and Training Committee for the New York City District Council of Carpenters
posting.unionName: New York City District Council of Carpenters
posting.applicationSummary: Attend a required in-person information session at the NYCDCC Training Center, then complete and submit the online application through the training center website within 60 days of the session.
posting.applicationHours: Information sessions are held the second Wednesday of each month from 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM, excluding December and January. No admittance after 9:00 AM.
posting.applicationLimitDetails: Spaces are limited and filled on a first-come, first-served basis. Applicants must sign in at the in-person information session to receive a passcode for the online application.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 17 years old at the time of application.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED. Proof is required after selection and before enrollment.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation to and from job sites and classes. Must be willing to travel to off-site training and accept the cost if training is canceled, missed, or not completed.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a substance abuse screening at the sponsor's expense within 48 hours of selection and before enrollment, at a designated collection site.
posting.classRequirement: Must be able to travel to and attend training and classes at the approved school and any off-site training sessions.
allRequirements: Must be at least 17 years old at the time of application.
Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED. Proof will be required after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must be physically able to perform the work of the trade without posing a direct threat to the safety of themselves or others.

Must be physically able to perform the work of a Carpenter, which may include working on scaffolds and in confined spaces, working in inclement weather such as rain, snow, cold, heat, and direct sunlight, performing constant repetitive motions, climbing and working from heights, lifting heavy items of at least 50 pounds, and standing and stooping for prolonged periods of time.
Must pass a substance abuse screening, at the sponsor's expense, within 48 hours of selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship, at a designated collection site designated by the JATC.
Must be able to read, hear, and understand verbal instructions and warnings in English.
Must provide proof with detailed examples of any trade-related experience, certification, or education after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must agree to sign an Apprentice handbook, subject to change at any time.

Must be willing to travel to off-site training and assume financial responsibility for associated costs in the event of cancellation, failure to attend, or failure to successfully complete the program.
Must be a U.S. citizen or have the legal right to work in the United States. Proof will be required after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must have reliable means of transportation to and from various job sites and classes at the approved school.
Must provide DD-214, Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty, if applicable, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 17, have a high school diploma or equivalency, pass a substance abuse screen, and be physically able to do demanding carpenter work. Reliable transportation, English comprehension, and eligibility to work in the U.S. are also required.
posting.contactName: New York City District Council of Carpenters
posting.contactSummary: For more information, contact the New York City District Council of Carpenters at (212) 727-2224.
